name: separator
description: A visual and semantic dividing line between content sections. Rendered as an <hr> element (horizontal or vertical) which carries role=separator implicitly in all browsers -- zero manual role attribute needed. Three line-stroke variants (default=solid, dashed, dotted). Optional centred label text replaces the <hr> with a flex-row [line][label][line] layout (the Ant Design Divider children use case). decorative=false by default (semantic); set decorative=true to hide the line from the a11y tree via role=presentation. No JS, no enhancer, no client state. CSP-clean. Tokens: --lv-color-border (line colour), --lv-border-width (stroke), --lv-space-4 (block margin), --lv-space-2 (inline margin for vertical), --lv-space-3 (label padding + flanking gap), --lv-text-sm + --lv-color-muted + --lv-font-sans + --lv-font-normal (label typography).
docs: Copies the separator partial to your JTE root at lievit/separator.jte, reached as @template.lievit.separator(). Renders an <hr> element whose role=separator is implicit -- no manual role attribute needed. Pass orientation="vertical" for an inline full-height rule in a flex row. Pass variant="dashed" or variant="dotted" to change the stroke style (default is solid). Pass label="OR" for the [line][label][line] layout; labelPosition="left" | "center" | "right" shifts the label (default center). Pass decorative=true to erase the element from the a11y tree (role=presentation) when the page structure already communicates the grouping. Extra STATIC author-typed HTML attributes via attrs ($unsafe, trusted raw -- never per-row DB values). The source is yours: edit freely.
